2604.16242.title: Detecting and Suppressing Reward Hacking with Gradient Fingerprints
2604.16242.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) typically optimizes for outcome rewards without imposing constraints on intermediate reasoning. This leaves training susceptible to reward hacking, where models exploit loopholes (e.g., spurious patterns in training data) in the reward function to achieve high scores without solving the intended task. These reward-hacking behaviors are often implicit, as the intermediate chain-of-thought (CoT) may appear plausible on the surface, limiting the effectiveness of purely text-based monitoring. We propose Gradient Fingerprint (GRIFT), a method for detecting reward hacking using models' internal computations. Given a prompt and a model-generated CoT, GRIFT computes gradients of the CoT conditioned on the prompt and compresses them into a compact representation, which is then used to assess whether the CoT reflects reward hacking behavior. Across verifiable reasoning benchmarks spanning math, code, and logical reasoning, GRIFT substantially outperforms strong baselines, including CoT Monitor and TRACE, achieving over 25% relative improvement in detecting reward hacking behavior. Moreover, integrating GRIFT into the rejection fine-tuning pipeline for reasoning tasks reduces reward hacking and improves performance on the true task objective. Our results highlight a promising direction of leveraging gradient level representations for assessing the quality of CoT reasoning traces. Our code is available at: https://github.com/songtao-x/reward_hack.
2604.16242.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習與可驗證獎勵（RLVR）通常優化結果獎勵，而不對中間推理施加約束。這使得訓練容易受到獎勵黑客行為的影響，模型利用獎勵函數中的漏洞（例如，訓練數據中的虛假模式）來獲得高分，而不解決預期的任務。這些獎勵黑客行為通常是隱性的，因為中間的思考鏈（CoT）表面上可能看起來合理，這限制了純文本監控的有效性。我們提出了梯度指紋（GRIFT），這是一種使用模型內部計算來檢測獎勵黑客的方法。給定一個提示和模型生成的 CoT，GRIFT 計算基於提示的 CoT 的梯度，並將其壓縮為緊湊的表示，然後用於評估該 CoT 是否反映獎勵黑客行為。在涵蓋數學、代碼和邏輯推理的可驗證推理基準上，GRIFT 顯著超越了強基準，包括 CoT 監控和 TRACE，在檢測獎勵黑客行為方面實現了超過 25% 的相對改進。此外，將 GRIFT 整合到推理任務的拒絕微調流程中可以減少獎勵黑客行為，並提高在真實任務目標上的性能。我們的結果突顯了利用梯度級表示來評估 CoT 推理痕跡質量的有前景方向。我們的代碼可在以下地址獲得：https://github.com/songtao-x/reward_hack。
2604.16242.author: Songtao Wang et.al.
2604.16242.authors: Songtao Wang, Quang Hieu Pham, Fangcong Yin, Xinpeng Wang, Jocelyn Qiaochu Chen, Greg Durrett, Xi Ye
